name: Paris Market
image_alt: Small round orange carrots in a cluster
description: A French heirloom carrot that grows as a round, golf-ball-sized root rather than the typical long tapered shape. Sweet and tender, ideal for container growing or shallow or rocky soils.
history: A 19th-century French market garden variety developed for growing in shallow Parisian market garden beds with little soil depth. The round shape was a practical solution to limited growing space.
seasonal_quirks: Matures quickly relative to standard carrots. Can be sown in succession every 3 weeks for continuous harvest. Handles hot weather better than most carrots when kept well watered.
harvest_cues: Roots should be 1.5–2 inches in diameter. Gently push soil aside to check. 55–65 days from seeding.
variety_notes: The round shape makes them easier to harvest cleanly in heavy soil. Excellent for children's gardens — easy to pull and fun to discover.
preservation: Short roots store well in moist sand. Pickle whole in vinegar brine.
